codeReview.summary: Core send/inbox/read/ack route handlers and tests were added, but the relay runtime entrypoint does not wire a messageStore, so the new messaging routes are effectively disabled in normal relay startup.

issues.description: createRelayServer is constructed with tokenVerifier and participantStore only, omitting messageStore. Because all new messaging routes are guarded by `&& messageStore` in server routing (`src/relay/server.ts` lines 237, 311, 321), `/messages`, `/inbox`, `/messages/:id/read`, and `/messages/:id/ack` are not available in the relay's actual runtime path, so VAL-RELAY-001/002/003 are not fully wired beyond test-only construction.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature metadata requires manual multi-identity transcript evidence, but the worker handoff reports only automated test commands while still marking `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json for `relay-async-send-inbox-read-ack-sync` includes verification step `Manual: multi-identity send/read/ack transcript with state snapshots`; handoff `verification.commandsRun` lists only test/typecheck/lint commands and no manual transcript artifacts.

summary: Failing review due to a blocking integration gap: relay runtime startup does not provide messageStore to createRelayServer, leaving the new async messaging endpoints disabled outside tests.
